page: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is also strong, with DDR5 memory bandwidth for small block read-only and large block write operations ranking in the top 10%. In cryptographic and vector-heavy workloads, the processor's support for AVX-512 and AES instructions enables top-tier performance in Passmark encryption and extended instruction tests. However, single-threaded performance is slightly lower, with gzip compression and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the middle 50%.

The instance is suited for compute-heavy multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations, as evidenced by top-tier Geekbench clang compilation scores and strong Passmark database operation results. It also demonstrates strong capabilities for large language model (LLM) inference, achieving top-tier text generation speeds for medium-sized models and strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models. A notable tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence, which may impact workloads dependent on high-speed local disk I/O.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory write bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES, driving top-tier results in cryptographic and software compilation tests. This profile makes it highly effective for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference, though workloads requiring high-speed local storage must rely on network-attached alternatives over its 38 Gbps baseline network interface.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.24xlarge features 96 AMD EPYC cores and 384 GB DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance.
tagline: A high-density AMD EPYC platform delivering top-tier multi-core performance and DDR5 memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory write bandwidth benchmarks
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
